hypothesis: Incorporating an active learning acquisition strategy that sequentially selects the next lattice structure experiment to minimize tensor reconstruction uncertainty—rather than passively accommodating whatever biased sampling an experimentalist happens to produce—will achieve equivalent predictive accuracy (R²) to uniform random sampling using significantly fewer total experimental observations, because tensor completion's low-rank structural assumptions enable reliable uncertainty quantification over unobserved tensor entries that can guide targeted sampling toward the most informative regions of the design space.
